ogTitle: Wedge Island vs Weetulta: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wedge Island and Weetulta.
heading: Wedge Island vs Weetulta — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wedge Island, South Australia and Weetulta, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Weetulta (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wedge Island or Weetulta safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wedge Island has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Weetulta scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wedge Island scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Weetulta scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wedge Island or Weetulta?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
